description: Letter from a woman who was the wife of Yehoshuaʿ al-Lādhiqī and the mother of Dosa b. Yehoshuaʿ, in al-Raqqa, to her son Dosa b. Yehoshuaʿ, in Fustat. In Judaeo-Arabic with the address in Arabic script. The address also identifies the sender as Sahl b. Ḥātim. Given that she states she is living with her daughter, Sahl b. Ḥātim is likely her son-in-law. She conveys her yearnings, "I am writing to you, my son, the dearest to me of all people, may God grant you length of days and perpetuate your eminence and His help to you and your happiness and your wellbeing and may He bestow much favor upon you, from the house in Raqqa, on Thursday, 21 Av [=August]. I am healthy; may God in His mercy grant you everlasting health. I have grown tired, my son, of writing you without receiving an answer, and I do not know why you make light of your family ties. From your brother, may God protect him, letters arrive all the time, but there is not a single letter from you among them. Yet you are in the same place. Don’t you know that when a letter comes from you, it is as if I see your face? Don’t you know that my heart yearns to receive news from you? What could be easier than writing a letter and easing my mind? Do not kill me before my time. [...] All I ask of you is to write me a letter from time to time, so that I will know you are healthy." (Frenkel's translation.) She also asks him to buy her certain items, including Isfahani kohl; tutty, both Indian and Marāzībī'; and a sky-blue veil (muʿayjir kuḥlī) with dark blue trimming (ṭarz zarqāʾ). (Information in part from Gil.)
tags: fasting, personal, threatening to die, mail-order medicine
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית מ-al-Raqqa ל-Darb Makīn (street), Fustat, Qaṣr al-Shamʿ (neighborhood). מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
